description: A U.S. Department of Labor designed graphic on a dark navy/black background. The top is a close-up photograph of President Donald Trump speaking in the Oval Office (American flags and gold drapery blurred behind him, dark suit with red tie and flag lapel pin). Across the middle in large bold white serif type reads the slogan 'AMERICANS FIRST'. Below is a white rounded-rectangle card designed to look like an embedded X/Twitter post from 'Rapid Response 47' (@RapidResponse47, with the official White House Rapid Response account branding, blue verified check, and small profile logo). The quoted post text reads: 'Since President Trump took office (Jan.-Sep. 2025): 2,57M native-born Americans GAINED employment; 1.03M foreign-born workers LOST employment. 2.64M native-born Americans JOINED the labor force; 1.23M foreign-born workers LEFT the labor force.' A small DOL seal is centered at the bottom. The technique pairs a presidential image with a re-shared official-account statistic to claim the administration's labor policies favor native-born workers. Target audience: American workers and the MAGA base, reinforcing the 'Americans First' / anti-foreign-worker jobs narrative.
notable_text: AMERICANS FIRST | Rapid Response 47 @RapidResponse47: Since President Trump took office (Jan.-Sep. 2025): 2.57M native-born Americans GAINED employment; 1.03M foreign-born workers LOST employment...
